fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue company with a high forward P/E, but currently unprofitable with negative margins and significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptionally strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current Q: 109.4% — very high expectations for near-term earnings.
Analyst growth est, current FY: 441.7% — massive growth projected for the full year.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may be outpacing valuation.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is not profitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor returns on capital.
Debt/Equity: 25.36 — very high leverage relative to equity.
Free cash flow (TTM): $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.
Last reported EPS -0.07 vs estimate -0.05 — missed expectations.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention recently, with articles highlighting substantial transceiver orders and upward revisions to analyst targets. Despite a recent earnings miss and negative margins, the company's revenue growth is exceptionally strong, and analyst estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year are extremely high. The stock's dramatic rise over the past six months suggests a narrative shift, though some analysts question if it's a bubble or a bottleneck.
